title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: You are given a Node.js Express application that uses a middleware function to validate route parameters. The middleware function checks if the route parameter is a positive integer or a string. However, there's a logical bug in the middleware function that allows invalid route parameters to pass through. Your task is to fix the bug and ensure that only valid route parameters are accepted.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the route parameter.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a boolean indicating whether the route parameter is valid or not.
statement.notes: The middleware function should be able to handle multiple path segments with route parameters.

hints.text: Check the type of the route parameter
Use a regular expression to match the route parameter

editorial.title: Editorial for Fixing Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: The middleware function should check the type of the route parameter and use a regular expression to match it.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve checking each path segment individually and validating its route parameter.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a regular expression to match the route parameter and then checking its type.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would involve using a regular expression to match the route parameter and then checking its type, with a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of path segments.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the regular expression matches the route parameter and then checking its type.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution would involve testing it with different inputs and checking its output.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the type of the route parameter
